one_line_value_proposition: Formally NICE-recommended digital CBT for insomnia — one of the most evidence-rich DTx in the NHS with multiple RCTs and a full NICE HTG recommendation.
why_it_matters_locally: Insomnia affects around a third of adults and is a common driver of GP appointments, prescription burden and mental health referrals. Sleepio provides a structured, fully digital CBT-I programme that can reach patients who cannot access face-to-face sleep clinics.
target_problem_statement: High demand for insomnia treatment, limited NHS sleep clinic capacity, over-reliance on hypnotic prescribing, and wide variation in access to evidence-based CBT-I.
evidence_summary: Multiple RCTs including the OASIS trial (n=1,711, general population, BMJ 2017). Formal NICE guidance via HTG624 (migrated from MTG70). Strong evidence base across general population, cancer, chronic pain and mental health comorbidity settings.

case_studies.title: OASIS trial — large-scale general population RCT
case_studies.outcome: Significant improvements in sleep quality, insomnia severity, anxiety and depression vs waitlist control. Effects maintained at 6-month follow-up.
case_studies.caveat: Digital self-help — outcomes represent population averages. Individual results vary. Big Health-affiliated funding for some research.

live_sites: Multiple NHS trusts, national deployments internationally. Formally recommended by NICE.
patients_covered_note: Millions of users globally. Deployed across multiple NHS organisations since NICE MTG70 recommendation.
dtac_note: DTAC status — confirm current status with Big Health before commissioning.
device_class: Not a regulated medical device for standard CBT-I pathway
gdpr_note: Data hosting — confirm with Big Health. US-based company — confirm data residency and DPA terms for NHS use.
target_patients: Adults with insomnia disorder or insomnia symptoms. Not for patients requiring immediate mental health crisis intervention.
exclusions: Not indicated for patients with severe mental illness, sleep apnoea as the primary cause, or where immediate clinical assessment is required.
onboarding_detail: Fully digital self-referral available. Patient accesses programme via web or app. Minimal clinical overhead.
supplier_wrap: Big Health provides digital programme, analytics and population-level reporting.
local_wraparound_detail: Low local effort required. Clinicians may need brief training on referral pathway and how to support patients who need escalation from digital CBT-I.
training_note: Minimal training required. Signposting guide for clinicians recommended.
monitoring_note: Self-management tool. Not a monitored clinical service. Appropriate escalation pathways for patients with worsening symptoms or mental health concerns must be defined locally.
escalation_note: Programme includes safety features and signposting. Local escalation pathways for mental health concerns should be communicated to patients.
operating_hours_caveat: Fully digital self-management programme — not a monitored service. Ensure patients know how to access clinical support if needed.
implementation_prerequisites: Referral pathway or self-referral process established
Clinician awareness of programme scope and limitations
indicative_price_text: Contact Big Health for NHS pricing. Some NHS organisations have accessed Sleepio via existing contracts or innovation funding.
service_wrap_note: Digital programme only. Clinical wraparound is commissioner/provider responsibility.
contract_note: Contact Big Health for NHS procurement options.
expected_benefit_note: Evidence shows significant improvement in sleep quality and reduction in insomnia severity. Potential to reduce GP appointments, hypnotic prescribing and mental health referrals attributable to poor sleep.
tariff_considerations: Insomnia is a common driver of GP and mental health appointments. Commissioners should model locally using appointment cost data and hypnotic prescribing volumes.
roi_note: Reduction in GP appointments and hypnotic prescribing is plausible based on trial evidence but local ROI modelling required.
nhse_125k_note: Not applicable — this is an insomnia product, not COPD.
demo_notes: Contact Big Health for a demonstration or trial access.
source_summary: NICE HTG624, Freeman et al. BMJ 2017, Big Health website.
confidence_note: Content based on publicly available information. Verify current assurance and pricing status with Big Health before use in procurement documentation.
monitoring_model: Self-management tool. Not a monitored clinical service.
minimum_conditions_for_success: Self-referral pathway or clinician referral process. Clinician awareness of programme scope. Minimum 200 patient cohort.
procurement_notes: Contact Big Health for NHS procurement options. Some NHS organisations have accessed via existing contracts.
cyber_notes: US-based company — confirm data residency and DPA terms for NHS use.

technical_integrations.data_hosting: Confirm with Big Health — US-based company

context_of_use.population: Adults with insomnia disorder or insomnia symptoms
context_of_use.therapeutic_purpose: Digital CBT for insomnia — structured 6-week programme
context_of_use.nice_scope: NICE HTG624 (migrated from MTG70) — formal recommendation
